description: §22.4.1 runtime (b) — `emit(html)` NORMALIZES escape sequences before the block splitter: a literal two-char `\n` (from the `\\n` source escape) is normalized to a real U+000A newline. The <pre> then holds a real newline between `a` and `b`, which the shared conformance normalizer collapses to a single space → textContent "a b". Sibling of meta-emit-raw-escape (verbatim → "a\nb"), pinning the emit()-vs-emit.raw() distinction.
rationale: §22.4.1: `emit(html)` SHALL normalize escape sequences (the `\n` table entry maps to U+000A LINE FEED) before the string is passed to the block splitter. The literal backslash-n in the value becomes a real newline in the rendered <pre>; the shared whitespace-collapsing normalizer renders that inter-character whitespace as a single space, so the textContent is "a b" (NOT the literal "a\nb" that emit.raw preserves).
